findings.evidence: Glob of code/TauCeti/Topology/Homotopy/ lists AmbientIsotopyConj.lean but no AmbientIsotopyGroup.lean; grep isotopicToId -> 'No files found'. Description: 'adding one new file ... AmbientIsotopyGroup.lean (146 lines)'.
findings.fix: Rewrite the description so the stated intent and dependencies match the actual content: this PR is the standalone conjugation-closure prerequisite (`conj`, `conj_finalHomeomorph`) of the general ambient-isotopy construction, not the normal subgroup. With the description corrected, scope is satisfied (single topic; genuine substrate per the encoding convention).
findings.issue: The PR description advertises the normal subgroup `TauCeti.isotopicToId`, its normality, `mem_isotopicToId_iff_ambientIsotopic`, and a 146-line `AmbientIsotopyGroup.lean` as the main results. None of these are in the diff or checkout — the only new file is `AmbientIsotopyConj.lean` (81 lines) defining `AmbientIsotopy.conj` and `conj_finalHomeomorph`. `grep isotopicToId` over ./code returns nothing; no AmbientIsotopyGroup.lean exists.

summary: The submitted code is a single coherent topic — only AmbientIsotopyConj.lean defining the conjugation closure `conj` — and it is genuine general ambient-isotopy substrate the roadmap's encoding convention mandates, so it is in scope. But the PR description describes a different, larger PR (the `isotopicToId` normal subgroup) that is absent from the diff, so the description must be fixed to match.
